name: Cerberus
shortDescription: Cerberus is the multi-headed dog that guards the gates of the Underworld, preventing the dead from leaving.
description: Cerberus, in Greek mythology, is the monstrous multi-headed dog, usually depicted with three heads, that guards the gates of the Underworld to prevent the dead from leaving. His appearance varies in different accounts, sometimes described with a mane of snakes, a serpent's tail, and lion's claws. Cerberus is the offspring of Typhon and Echidna, making him a sibling to other famous monsters like the Chimera, Hydra, and Sphinx. His primary role is to ensure that no soul escapes Hades' realm. He is known for his ferocity and terrifying bark. One of the Labors of Heracles involved capturing Cerberus, which the hero accomplished through strength and guile, sometimes with the aid of Hermes and Athena. Cerberus represents the inescapable nature of death and the boundaries between the world of the living and the dead.
appearance: Multi-headed dog, typically three heads, sometimes with a serpent's tail and/or a mane of snakes.
abilities: Guarding the gates of the Underworld
